summary: Russian tactical aviation launched guided bombs on Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia regions. Multiple rockets struck Vinnytsia, Cherkasy and Bila Tserkva, while drones attacked several northern oblasts and a Romanian city. Ukraine reported a successful strike on a Russian FSB electronic‑recon site in the Krasnodar region.
key_points: Two rockets struck Bila Tserkva, Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67278).
A Russian drone crashed into a building in Galaţi, Romania, prompting diplomatic protest (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30515).
Russian forces destroyed a school in Novhorod‑Siverskyi district, Chernihiv region, with a Geranium drone (Source: @UkraineNow/81526).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Donetsk (Source: @kpszsu/63263) and Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/63264) oblasts.
High threat of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67272).
Possible combined strike involving 25 Kh‑101 cruise missiles from Tu‑95MS and Tu‑160 bombers, plus up to 24 Kalibr missiles from the Black Sea (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67298).

sections.executive_summary: Russian tactical aviation dropped guided bombs on Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia, while multiple rocket salvos hit Vinnytsia and Bila Tserkva. Drones intensified activity across northern oblasts, including a strike that destroyed a school in Chernihiv and a crash in Galaţi, Romania. Ukraine claimed a successful strike on a Russian FSB electronic‑recon node in the Krasnodar region.

points: The Security Service of Ukraine (SBU) neutralised a Russian FSB radio‑electronic intelligence node in the Krasnodar region, which had been used to guide missiles and drones over Ukrainian territory (Source: @UkraineNow/81537).

Ukrainian officials reported that Russian drones repeatedly attacked Naftogaz facilities in Kharkiv and Sumy oblasts, with attacks lasting almost a full day and some targets hit multiple times (Source: @UkraineNow/81534).
A Russian drone struck a multi‑storey residential building in Galaţi, Romania, causing a fire, two injuries and the evacuation of 70 residents (Source: @UkraineNow/81524).

Ukrainian forces observed increased activity of Russian transport aircraft AN‑72 and IL‑76MD‑90A heading to Kursk and Shaykovka airfields, suggesting preparation for larger weapon deliveries (Source: @eRadarrua/94249).
Ukrainian leadership highlighted difficulties in replenishing low‑altitude air‑defence systems, noting a gap between Gepard losses and limited refurbishment efforts (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30512).

Russian tactical aviation conducted guided‑bomb releases over Donetsk (Source: @kpszsu/63263) and Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/63264) oblasts, indicating continued air‑strike capability in contested zones.
Russian forces deployed reactive UAVs along the northern border of Chernihiv and Kyiv oblasts, flying westward from the Belarusian border (Source: @kpszsu/63290).
A high alert was issued for possible Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from the Bryansk region, reflecting heightened strategic missile readiness (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67272).
Russian naval aviation reportedly launched a swarm of small UAVs from multiple locations, though numbers were limited (Source: @eRadarrua/94248).
Russian air‑defence units were observed positioning near the Black Sea coast, with UAVs originating from the Black Sea heading toward Odesa region (Source: @kpszsu/63276).
Three UAV groups were reported over Kharkiv oblast heading toward Valki from the north (Source: @eRadarrua/94231).
UAVs from the Belgorod region crossed the Kharkiv‑Sumy border moving westward across Kharkiv (Source: @kpszsu/63281).
A reactive UAV approached Chernihiv from the northeast (Source: @kpszsu/63291).
UAV activity was noted near the northern part of Dnipropetrovsk oblast, moving southward (Source: @kpszsu/63281).
Guided‑bomb strikes were recorded in Donetsk, indicating continued Russian air operations on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/63263).
Two rockets entered Vinnytsia region from an unspecified direction (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67269).
Two rockets struck Bila Tserkva, located north of Kyiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67278).
Reactive UAVs were observed along the northern border of Chernihiv oblast, flying westward along the Belarus border (Source: @kpszsu/63290).
A UAV group was reported over Chernihiv heading toward Snovsk, prompting local alerts (Source: @eRadarrua/94226).
UAVs were sighted over Kyiv oblast moving westward from the north (Source: @kpszsu/63290).
UAVs launched from the Black Sea were observed heading toward Odesa region (Source: @kpszsu/63276).
Multiple UAVs from the Black Sea were reported moving toward Chornomorsk (Source: @kpszsu/63281).
A Russian drone attacked a residential building in Galaţi, Romania, after crossing the airspace over Odesa (Source: @UkraineNow/81524).
Potential combined strike plans mentioned the use of up to 24 Kalibr missiles launched from the Black Sea (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67298).
Ukrainian forces reported increased Russian UAV activity over the southern oblasts, though specific numbers were not provided (Source: @eRadarrua/94248).
Russian tactical aviation released guided aviation bombs over Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia oblasts (Sources: @kpszsu/63263, @kpszsu/63264).
Multiple rocket salvos were recorded hitting Vinnytsia, Cherkasy and Bila Tserkva (Sources: @mon1tor_ua/67269, @mon1tor_ua/67264, @mon1tor_ua/67270).
High‑alert warnings issued for possible Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67272).
Russian transport aircraft AN‑72 and IL‑76MD‑90A were observed preparing for possible large‑scale weapon deliveries (Source: @eRadarrua/94249).
UAV activity intensified across northern and eastern fronts, with several groups reported moving from Belarus, the Black Sea and internal Russian territories (Various sources).

key_developments: Two rockets struck Vinnytsia, causing damage in the region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67269).
Two rockets hit Bila Tserkva, indicating continued missile pressure on central Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67278).
A Russian drone crashed into a building in Galaţi, Romania, prompting diplomatic condemnation (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30515).
Russian forces destroyed a school in Novhorod‑Siverskyi district, Chernihiv region, with a Geranium drone (Source: @UkraineNow/81526).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia oblasts (Sources: @kpszsu/63263, @kpszsu/63264).
High threat level announced for Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67272).
Intelligence suggests a possible combined strike using 25 Kh‑101 cruise missiles from Tu‑95MS/Tu‑160 bombers and up to 24 Kalibr missiles from the Black Sea (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67298).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian air and missile activity remains intense across multiple fronts, with guided‑bomb strikes in the east, rocket salvos in central Ukraine, and heightened ballistic‑missile readiness from Bryansk. UAV operations are proliferating, targeting both civilian infrastructure and military sites, including cross‑border incidents in Romania. Ukraine's air‑defence gaps, especially at low altitude, increase vulnerability to the growing drone threat.
sections.regional_response: Romania has issued diplomatic protests and is monitoring Russian UAV incursions after the Galaţi crash. Ukrainian authorities continue to issue local alerts and mobilise air‑defence assets, while also conducting counter‑strikes on Russian electronic‑recon nodes. International partners are monitoring the escalation but have not announced new immediate measures.
sections.intelligence_notes: The convergence of guided‑bomb deployments, rocket salvos, and potential large‑scale missile launches suggests a coordinated escalation by Russian forces. UAV traffic from the Black Sea and Belarus indicates a multi‑axis approach to pressure Ukrainian air‑defence. Monitoring of Russian transport aircraft movements to Kursk and Shaykovka airfields may foretell further weapon deliveries.
